doc_section:
## Sending Emails
Call `send_email(to, subject, body)` to send an email. If sending fails, it will not retry — you must handle retries yourself.
why: Doc explicitly contradicts new retry behavior.

new_code:
def calculate_total(items):
    return sum(item.price for item in items)
doc_section:
## Calculating Totals
`calculate_total(items)` returns the sum of all item prices in the list.
why: Internal refactor only, no behavior or signature change visible to doc.

doc_section:
## Creating Users
Use `create_user(name, email, is_admin=False)` to create a user. Pass `is_admin=True` to create an admin directly.
why: Parameter removed entirely; documented usage would now raise a TypeError.

doc_section:
## Fetching Items
`fetch_items()` returns a page of items. By default, results are paginated in batches.
why: Doc doesn't mention the specific default number, so technically still accurate, but arguably incomplete now. Good test of over-flagging tendency.

doc_section:
## Payments
When an order is processed, a receipt is generated immediately after the card is charged.
why: Doc no longer holds for high-value orders (no receipt if flagged for review). Section never names process_payment directly — tests embedding-fallback-style reasoning even in this pre-linking spike.

doc_section:
## Calculating Totals
`calculate_total(items)` returns the sum of all item prices in the list.
why: Test-file change only — in the real pipeline this would be filtered before ever reaching the LLM (Milestone 2), but useful here to confirm the model itself doesn't over-flag irrelevant changes.
